AboutSection.title: About Us
AboutSection.text: The Drought and Desertification Early Warning System (SAP) is the instrument that operationalizes the legal mandate to establish an integrated early warning information system for drought events and desertification processes. Under Law No. 13,153/2015, this duty is assigned to the public sector within the framework of the National Policy for Combating Desertification and Mitigating the Effects of Drought (PNCD). The SAP provides integrated and actionable information to plan, prioritize, and respond to risks. The system enables the core objectives of the PNCD, transforming observations and indicators into alerts and evidence for contingency decisions, adaptation, livelihood protection, and resource allocation.
